verses.text: The LORD spake unto Moses: speak unto the children of Israel that they bring Me an offering — of every man that giveth it willingly with his heart shall ye take My offering. Guidance: God only takes what is given willingly. The first principle of tabernacle-building is heart-giving. He could command it; instead He waits to be asked-into.

The LORD spake unto Moses: speak unto the children of Israel that they bring Me an offering — of every man that giveth it willingly with his heart shall ye take My offering. Guidance: God only takes what is given willingly. The first principle of tabernacle-building is heart-giving. He could command it; instead He waits to be asked-into.

This is the offering: gold, silver, brass; blue, purple, scarlet, fine linen, goats' hair; rams' skins dyed red, badgers' skins, shittim wood; oil for light, spices for anointing and incense; onyx stones and stones for the ephod and breastplate. Guidance: Israel arrives at Sinai with the spoils of Egypt. What once adorned their oppressor now builds a house for God. Every former-treasure becomes new-purpose.

Let them make Me a sanctuary, that I may dwell among them. According to all I shew thee — the pattern of the tabernacle, the pattern of all the instruments — even so shall ye make it. Guidance: God dwells in the camp He just rescued. The pattern comes down from heaven; Moses doesn't design. Every detail is given — because God's house is built His way.

Let them make Me a sanctuary, that I may dwell among them. According to all I shew thee — the pattern of the tabernacle, the pattern of all the instruments — even so shall ye make it. Guidance: God dwells in the camp He just rescued. The pattern comes down from heaven; Moses doesn't design. Every detail is given — because God's house is built His way.

Make an ark of shittim wood, two and a half cubits long, a cubit and a half wide. Overlay with pure gold within and without; make a gold crown. Cast four rings to the corners; make staves overlaid with gold to bear it; the staves shall not be removed. Put the testimony inside. Guidance: First built, deepest hidden. Wood within, gold within and without. Staves stay attached — this God moves with His people.

Make a mercy seat of pure gold, two and a half by one and a half cubits. Two cherubim of beaten gold at the ends, wings stretched, faces toward each other. Set it above the ark; put the testimony inside. There I will meet thee — commune above the mercy seat between the cherubim. Guidance: God's address: above the mercy seat, between the cherubim. Mercy covers the law within. Where God meets His people is at atonement.

Make a table of shittim wood, two cubits long, one wide, a cubit and a half high. Overlay with pure gold, gold crown round about; a handbreadth border with its crown. Four gold rings hold staves. Dishes, spoons, covers, bowls — pure gold. Set shewbread on the table before Me always. Guidance: Twelve loaves, one per tribe, set continually before the LORD. His people are always-present at His table. He calls Himself the bread of life.

Make a candlestick of pure gold, beaten work — shaft, branches, bowls, knops, flowers all one piece. Six branches at the sides, three each. Almond-shaped bowls with knop and flower on each branch; four on the shaft. Light its seven lamps. Tongs and snuffdishes of pure gold; a talent of gold. Make all after the pattern shewed thee. Guidance: A tree of beaten gold, seven lamps, almond blossoms — the only light in the holy place. Christ: I am the light of the world.
